findings.message: The DLT streaming pipeline has a logic issue in `silver_events`: `parse_event_payload(F.col('value'))` is applied without first selecting/carrying the `value` column from `bronze_df` into the projection that `parsed.alias('event')` is built from. As written, this is likely to fail or be evaluated incorrectly in Spark because `parsed` is not tied to the input DataFrame row context.
findings.suggested_fix: Build the parsed struct from `bronze_df.select(F.from_json(F.col('value').cast('string'), EVENT_SCHEMA).alias('event'), ...)` or use `bronze_df.select('*', parse_event_payload(F.col('value')).alias('event'))` before referencing nested fields.

findings.message: The tests rely on lightweight stubs and only validate chained-call construction, not runtime Spark/DLT lifecycle behavior or schema parsing semantics.
findings.suggested_fix: Add integration-style tests with a real Spark session (or higher-fidelity local fixtures) to validate the parsed columns, expectations, and streaming aggregation behavior end-to-end.

findings.message: There are unused imports in the test file (`datetime`, `Row`) and in the implementation (`Dict`).
findings.suggested_fix: Remove unused imports to reduce noise and keep linting clean.

summary: Core structure is reasonable, but `silver_events` likely has a Spark expression composition bug and test coverage is too stub-heavy to validate streaming behavior.
